mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing the present value of corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC > 0.05 consistently across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise together, or the interaction term shows negative coefficient in multivariate regression over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple rolling windows, or returns are negative during strong payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth rate surpasses 3.5%, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally during high inflation periods with CPIAUCSL growth above 3.5%, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales outpace GDP expansion, signals accelerating consumer demand relative to broader output, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The retail sales to GDP growth ratio shows no predictive power for equity returns, or the interaction effect reverses sign during periods of supply chain constraints.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in housing starts indicate a collapsing residential investment cycle and weakening construction sector demand, which reduces capital goods orders and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative equity returns, or SPX shows positive returns in > 60% of instances following three-month housing declines.
